subtitle: Change Agent · AI-Enhanced Delivery
hero_sub: I diagnose team dysfunction, coach through resistance, and drive accountability. Outcomes, not process compliance.

the_case.need: Proven success leading teams through organizational change
the_case.answer: Eptura: Agile adoption from scratch across Marketing. A&E: cultural transformation from Post-its to structured Jira delivery. Underline: sold Scrum to a skeptical leadership team and delivered in 16 weeks.
the_case.detail: Organizational change isn't a workshop — it's a sustained campaign. I've done it at Eptura (org-wide Agile mentorship, 35%+ efficiency gain), A&E (migrated 10 teams from manual tracking to Jira over 2 years), and Underline (proposed, sold, and delivered Scrum adoption in a company that had never used it).

the_case.need: Demonstrated ability to address low accountability, performance challenges, team dysfunction
the_case.answer: Distressed program recovery at Understood.org. Low-accountability team coaching at Eptura. Resistance navigation at Underline and A&E.
the_case.detail: I've taken over programs in their final months with broken team dynamics and delivered on-time launches. I've coached teams where the real impediment wasn't technical — it was accountability and trust. I address negative behaviors directly, constructively, and early.

the_case.need: AI tools to enhance Agile delivery — as a thought partner, not just a tool
the_case.answer: Jira AI and Microsoft Copilot embedded at Crocs (~30% overhead reduction). Six production applications built using AI-assisted development. AI used in planning, synthesis, and decision support across every recent engagement.
the_case.detail: I've been using AI tools in active delivery workflows since 2022. At Crocs I embedded Jira AI and Copilot into release governance, reducing administrative overhead by ~30% while improving reporting quality. I build production software using Claude Code and ChatGPT. 'Thought partner and capability multiplier' is exactly how I use these tools.

the_case.need: Strong facilitation, conflict resolution, servant leadership
the_case.answer: 10+ years facilitating ceremonies with a focus on outcomes, not completion. Conflict resolution across regulated, high-pressure, globally distributed teams.
the_case.detail: I facilitate for outcomes, not activity. Every standup ends with decisions made and blockers owned. Retrospectives produce actual workflow changes, not just conversation. I resolve conflict directly — I don't let it fester until it affects delivery.

the_case.need: Influencing cross-functional teams without direct authority
the_case.answer: Every SM role I've held has been influence without authority. Eptura PMO: influenced 10 teams with no direct reports. DigitasLBI: influenced a regulated financial-services program as an embedded agency SM.
the_case.detail: Influence without authority is the SM's entire job. I do it through trust, consistency, and demonstrated credibility — not personality. At Eptura I influenced org-wide Agile adoption from the PMO without any management authority over the teams I was coaching.

the_case.need: Data and metrics to identify trends and drive improvement
the_case.answer: Built standardized Agile metrics at Eptura (velocity, burndown, cycle time, team health). Delivery dashboards in ADO, Jira, and Smartsheet across multiple programs.
the_case.detail: Metrics are a coaching tool, not a reporting obligation. I build dashboards that surface patterns, not just status. At Eptura I used delivery data to identify specific team dysfunctions and target coaching interventions — which produced the 35%+ efficiency gain.

the_stack.headline: The delivery ecosystem.
the_stack.subhead: The tools I use to run clean delivery and surface what matters.

nodes.label: Jira & Jira AI
nodes.desc: Primary delivery tracking: epics, stories, sprint boards, dashboards, burndown, velocity
nodes.pm_role: Configure and maintain board structure, workflow automation, and reporting. Use Jira AI for backlog synthesis, sprint summaries, and decision documentation.

nodes.label: Azure DevOps (ADO)
nodes.desc: Enterprise delivery tracking and release management at Eptura, Understood.org, and Crocs
nodes.pm_role: Board configuration, sprint tracking, delivery health dashboards, and dependency visualization across teams.

nodes.label: Microsoft Copilot
nodes.desc: AI-assisted reporting, stakeholder communication drafting, and meeting synthesis
nodes.pm_role: Reduces administrative overhead on status reports, retrospective summaries, and stakeholder updates — freeing coaching time for actual coaching.

nodes.label: Claude / AI Development
nodes.desc: AI-assisted software development and delivery workflow automation
nodes.pm_role: Used in planning, synthesis, workflow analysis, and to build six production applications demonstrating genuine technical depth.

nodes.label: Confluence & Smartsheet
nodes.desc: Documentation, process frameworks, and program-level reporting
nodes.pm_role: Delivery health templates, team health frameworks, retrospective-to-action tracking, and leadership reporting infrastructure.

when_things_break.scenario: Team member consistently misses sprint commitments — pattern, not one-off
response: Distinguish between capacity issue, clarity issue, or accountability issue before acting
Bring it into the team if it's a systemic issue; keep it private if it's individual
Adjust sprint planning to surface the pattern earlier, not just later
Escalate to leadership if 2 sprint cycles don't show change — with documentation

when_things_break.scenario: Retrospective produces zero actionable items for the third consecutive sprint
response: Change the format — if the same structure produces no results, the structure is the problem
Introduce a different facilitation technique: silent writing, dot voting, 1-2-4-All
Name the pattern explicitly with the team — 'we've done this three times with no output'
Investigate what psychological safety issue is suppressing honest feedback
Commit to one specific change per retro minimum, measurable by next sprint

when_things_break.scenario: Engineering and Product are in open conflict over scope mid-sprint
response: Stop the argument — get facts on the table before positions harden
Map what was committed at sprint planning vs. what is being asked now
Facilitate the trade-off decision explicitly: scope, time, or quality — someone decides
Document the decision and who made it — removes ambiguity for the next conflict
Retrospective: what process failure allowed this conflict to emerge mid-sprint?

how_i_run_a_room: Ceremonies end with decisions made — not deferred to 'follow-up'
Retrospectives produce at least one measurable workflow change per sprint
Action items are owned with names and dates before the meeting ends
Impediments are removed within 24 hours or escalated with a documented reason
Status updates go out proactively — leadership never has to ask
Metrics are used to coach, not to report — patterns drive interventions
Difficult conversations happen immediately, not at the next scheduled touchpoint

points: Spearheaded Agile adoption across Marketing department — zero prior Agile practice
Coached ~10 distributed teams through behavioral and cultural transformation
35%+ delivery efficiency gain; 20% sprint carryover reduction
Mentored junior Scrum Masters across the organization
Distressed program recovery: took over in final crucial months
Rebuilt team health, accountability, and stakeholder confidence
Delivered on-time platform replatform with no critical regressions
Advised VP of Engineering on organizational change management strategy
Led full cultural transformation: Post-it notes to structured Jira delivery
Coached ~10 concurrent teams over 2 years toward self-organizing delivery
Coached Product Owners on backlog accountability and sprint discipline
Shipped all SVOD platforms against externally visible launch windows
Proposed and sold Scrum to leadership team with zero Agile background
Recruited and contracted distributed dev team; ran all delivery from scratch
Enterprise SaaS platform upgrade delivered in 16 weeks, on time and under budget

before: Teams are meeting, but decisions aren't being made and commitments aren't being kept
You're the de facto conflict resolver because the SM isn't handling it
Metrics exist but don't drive any actual behavior change
Organizational change initiatives stall because no one is coaching through the resistance
after: Teams self-organize around commitments — you're not the accountability backstop
Conflicts surface and resolve at the team level before they reach leadership
Metrics drive targeted coaching interventions that produce measurable improvement
Change adoption accelerates because someone is actively coaching through resistance, not just facilitating around it

the_ramp.deliverable: Shadow assigned teams. Map the real team dynamics — what's said vs. what's actually happening. Identify the top 3 impediments and accountability gaps.
Facilitate first full set of ceremonies. Introduce one process improvement. Begin 1:1s with team members. Surface patterns to leadership.
First retrospective with measurable output. Direct conversations underway where needed. Delivery metrics baseline established.
Teams are measurably more accountable. At least one systemic issue identified, addressed, and tracked. Leadership has clear visibility into team health.
